\"\"\"\n2: Core module responsible for converting Python bytecode into TorchDynamo's symbolic execution format.\n3: \n4: This module implements the bytecode-level tracing system that allows TorchDynamo to analyze\n5: and transform Python code. It converts Python bytecode instructions into a symbolic format\n6: that tracks the flow of tensors and other values through the program.\n7: \n8: Key components:\n9: - InstructionTranslatorBase: Base class for converting bytecode to symbolic execution\n10: - InstructionTranslator: Main translator for function bytecode\n11: - InliningInstructionTranslator: Handles inlining of called functions\n12: - SpeculationLog: Manages state for speculative execution and rollback\n13: \n14: The symbolic conversion process handles:\n15: - Control flow (loops, conditionals, etc.)\n16: - Function inlining and \n829:     def test_autocast_graph_break_method(self):\n830:         class MyModule(torch.nn.Module):\n831:             def __init__(self, bias):\n832:                 super().__init__()\n833: )\n865:             self.assertEqual(res.dtype, torch.float32)\n866: \n867:         real_16, real_32 = module(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n868:         real_device_16 = real_16.device\n869:        setup_fn_target_offsets: tuple[int, ...],\\n580:         *args: Any,\\n581:     ) -> types.CodeType:\\n582:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n583:         This handles the case of generating a resume into code generated\\n584:         to resume something else.  We want to always generate starting\\n585:         from the original code object so that if control flow paths\\n586:         converge we only generated 1 resume function (rather than 2^n\\n587:         resume functions).\\n588:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n589: \\n590:         meta: ResumeFunctionMetadata = ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[\\n591:             code\\n592:         ]\\n593:         new_offset = -1\\n594: \\n595:         def find_new_offset(\\n596:             instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\\n597:         ) -> None:\\n598:             nonlocal new_offset\\n599:             (target,) = (i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\\n600:             # match the functions starting at the last instruction as we have added a prefix\\n601:             (new_target,) = (\\n602:                 i2\\n603:                 for i1, i2 in zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions))\\n604:                 if i1 is target\\n605:             )\\n606:             assert target.opcode == new_target.opcode\\n607:             assert new_target.offset is not None\\n608:             new_offset = new_target.offset\\n609: \\n610:         transform_code_object(code, find_new_offset)\\n611:         assert new_offset >= 0\\n612: \\n613:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\\n614:             # setup_fn_target_offsets currently contains the target offset of\\n615:             # each setup_fn, based on `code`. state across execution boundaries\\n8: - Transforming and generating new code objects with preserved execution state\\n9: - Supporting Python 3.11+ exception handling and block management\\n10: - state.\\n14: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n15: \\n16: import copy\\n17: import dataclasses\\n18: import sys\\n19: import types\\n20: from collections.abc import Iterable\\n21: from contextlib import We want to always generate starting\\n585:         from the original code object so that if control flow paths\\n586:         converge we only generated 1 resume function (rather than 2^n\\n587:         resume functions).\\n588:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n589: \\n590:         meta: ResumeFunctionMetadata = ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[\\n591:             code\\n592:         ]\\n593:         new_offset = -1\\n594: \\n595:         def find_new_offset(\\n596:             instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\\n597:         ) -> None:\\n598:             nonlocal new_offset\\n599:             (target,) = (i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\\n600:             # match the functions starting at the last instruction as we have added a prefix\\n601:             (new_target,) = (\\n602:                 i2\\n603:                 for i1, i2 in zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions))\\n604:                 if i1 is target\\n605:             )\\n606:             assert target.opcode == new_target.opcode\\n607:             assert new_target.offset is not None\\n608:             new_offset = new_target.offset\\n609: \\n610:         transform_code_object(code, find_new_offset)\\n611:         assert new_offset >= 0\\n612: \\n613:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\\n614:             # setup_fn_target_offsets currently contains the target offset of\\n615:             # each setup_fn, based on `code`. When we codegen the resume function\\n616:             # based on the original code object, `meta.code`, the offsets in\\n617:             # setup_fn_target_offsets must be based on `meta.code` instead.\\n618:             if new_offset not in meta.block_target_offset_remap:\\n619:                 block_target_offset_remap = meta.block_target_offset_remap[\\n620:                     new_offset\\n621:                 ] = {}\\n622: \\n623:                 def remap_block_offsets(\\n624:                     instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\\n625:                 ) -> None:\\n626:                     # NOTE: each prefix block generates exactly one PUSH_EXC_INFO,\\n627:                     # so we can tell which block a prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO belongs to,\\n628:                     # by counting. Then we can use meta.prefix_block-target_offset_remap\\n629:                     # to determine where in the original code the PUSH_EXC_INFO offset\\n630:                     # replaced.\\n631:                     prefix_blocks: list[Instruction] = []\\n632:                     for inst in instructions:\\n633:                         if len(prefix_blocks) == len(\\n634:                             meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\\n635:                         ):\\n636:                             break\\n637:                         if inst.opname == \\\"PUSH_EXC_INFO\\\":\\n638:                             prefix_blocks.append(inst)\\n639: \\n640:                     # offsets into prefix\\n641:                     for inst, o in zip(\\n642:                         prefix_blocks, meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\\n643:                     ):\\n644:                         block_target_offset_remap[cast(int, inst.offset)] = o\\n645: \\n646:                     # old bytecode targets are after the prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO's\\n647:                     old_start_offset = (\\n648:                         cast(int, prefix_blocks[-1].offset) if prefix_blocks else -1\\n649:                     )\\n650:                     # offsets into old bytecode\\n651:                     old_inst_offsets = sorted(\\n652:                         n for n in setup_fn_target_offsets if n > old_start_offset\\n653:                     )\\n654:                     targets = _filter_iter(\\n655:                         instructions, old_inst_offsets, lambda inst, o: inst.offset == o\\n656:                     )\\n657:                     new_targets = _filter_iter(\\n658:                         zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions)),\\n659:                         targets,\\n660:                         lambda v1, v2: v1[0] is v2,\\n661:                     )\\n662:                     for new, old in zip(new_targets, targets):\\n663:                         block_target_offset_remap[old.offset] = new[1].offset\\n664: \\n665:                 transform_code_object(code, remap_block_offsets)\\n666: \\n667:             # if offset is not in setup_fn_target_offsets, it is an error\\n668:             setup_fn_target_offsets = tuple(\\n669:                 meta.block_target_offset_remap[new_offset][n]\\n670:                 for n in setup_fn_target_offsets\\n671:             )\\n672:         return ContinueExecutionCache.lookup(\\n673:             meta.code, lineno, new_offset, setup_fn_target_offsets, *args\\n674:         )\\n675: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/pytorch--cbe1a35d@26a1b9cce29f2edfbed661a229ead67fed613914/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/resume_execution.py\"}","structuredContent":{"content":"1: \"\"\"\n2: This module provides functionality for resuming Python execution at specific points in code,\n3: primarily used by PyTorch Dynamo for control flow handling and optimization. It implements\n4: bytecode transformation and execution state management to enable:\n5: \n6: - Resuming execution at arbitrary points in Python bytecode\n7: - Managing context managers and their state across execution boundaries\n8: - Transforming and generating new code objects with preserved execution state\n9: - Supporting Python 3.11+ exception handling and block management\n10: - Restoring torch function mode stacks and other execution context\n11: \n12: The module is critical for PyTorch Dynamo's ability to optimize code while preserving\n13: Python semantics and execution state.\n14:  Two-pointer conditional filter.\n    e.g. _filter_iter(insts, sorted_offsets, lambda i, o: i.offset == o)\n    returns the instructions with offsets in sorted_offsets\n    \"\"\"\n    it = iter(l2)\n  Two-pointer conditional filter.\n    e.g. _filter_iter(insts, sorted_offsets, lambda i, o: i.offset == o)\n    returns the instructions with offsets in sorted_offsets\n    \"\"\"\n    it = iter(l2)\n          create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=True),\n                    create_instruction(\n                        \"STORE_FAST\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\n                    ),\n                ]\n            )\n\n            cleanup: list[Instruction] = []\n            hooks = {fn.stack_index: fn for fn in setup_fns}\n            hook_target_offsets = {\n                fn.stack_index: setup_fn_target_offsets[i]\n                for i, fn in enumerate(setup_fns)\n            }\n            offset_to_inst = {inst.offset: inst for inst in instructions}\n            # map old hook targets to new targets generated by the hook\n            old_hook_target_remap = {}\n            null_idxes_i = 0\n            stack_ctx_vars_d = dict(stack_ctx_vars)  # type: ignore[var-annotated,arg-type]\n            for i in range(nstack):\n                while (\n                    null_idxes_i < len(null_idxes)\n                    and null_idxes[null_idxes_i] == i + null_idxes_i\n                ):\n                    prefix.append(create_instruction(\"PUSH_NULL\"))\n                    null_idxes_i += 1\n                prefix.append(create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=f\"___stack{i}\"))\n                if i in hooks:\n                    hook = hooks.pop(i)\n                    hook_insts, exn_target = hook(code_options, cleanup)\n                    prefix.extend(hook_insts)\n                    if is_py311_plus:\n                        hook_target_offset = hook_target_offsets.pop(i)\n                        old_hook_target = offset_to_inst[hook_target_offset]\n                        meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap.append(hook_target_offset)\n                        old_hook_target_remap[old_hook_target] = exn_target\n                if i in stack_ctx_vars_d:\n                    # NOTE: we assume that current stack var is a context manager CLASS!\n                    # Load args for context variable and construct it\n                    prefix.extend(_load_tuple_and_call(stack_ctx_vars_d[i]))\n\n            if is_py311_plus:\n                # reverse the mapping since targets of later/nested contexts are inserted\n                # into the mapping later, but show up earlier in the prefix.\n                meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap = list(\n                    reversed(meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap)\n                )\n\n            assert not hooks\n\n            # NOTE: we assume that local var is a context manager CLASS!\n            # initialize inactive context vars in argnames\n            for name, vals in argnames_ctx_vars:\n                prefix.append(create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=name))\n                prefix.extend(_load_tuple_and_call(vals))\n                prefix.append(create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=name))\n\n            # 3.12+: store NULL into variables that were NULL\n            if argnames_null:\n                assert sys.version_info >= (3, 12)\n                for v in argnames_null:\n                    assert v not in args\n                    prefix.extend(\n                        [\n                            create_instruction(\"PUSH_NULL\"),\n                            create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=v),\n                        ]\n                    )\n\n            # Call nested resume function\n            if nested_code_objs:\n                prefix.extend(\n                    [\n                        # set up __nested_resume_fns[-1] call\n                        *add_push_null(\n                            [\n                                create_instruction(\n                                    \"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"\n                                ),\n                                create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n                                create_instruction(\"BINARY_SUBSCR\"),\n                            ]\n                        ),\n                        # del __nested_resume_fns[-1]\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"),\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n                        create_instruction(\"DELETE_SUBSCR\"),\n                        # load [__nested_resume_fns, __nested_frame_values]\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"),\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_frame_values\"),\n                        create_instruction(\"BUILD_LIST\", arg=2),\n                        # load __nested_frame_values[-1]\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_frame_values\"),\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n                        create_instruction(\"BINARY_SUBSCR\"),\n                        # create [\n                        #     __nested_resume_fns,\n                        #     __nested_frame_values,\n                        #     *__nested_frame_values[-1],\n                        # ]\n                        create_instruction(\"LIST_EXTEND\", arg=1),\n                        # del __nested_frame_values[-1]\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_frame_values\"),\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n                        create_instruction(\"DELETE_SUBSCR\"),\n                        # delete __nested values\n                        create_instruction(\"DELETE_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"),\n                        create_instruction(\n                            \"DELETE_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_frame_values\"\n                        ),\n                        # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue back to allow graph breaks\n                        # in the nested resume\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=False),\n                        create_instruction(\n                            \"STORE_FAST\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\n                        ),\n                        # finish the call\n                        create_instruction(\"CALL_FUNCTION_EX\", arg=0),\n                    ]\n                )\n            else:\n                # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue back to allow graph breaks after the jump\n                prefix.extend(\n                    [\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=False),\n                        create_instruction(\n                            \"STORE_FAST\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\n                        ),\n                    ]\n                )\n\n            prefix.append(create_jump_absolute(target))\n\n            # because the line number table monotonically increases from co_firstlineno\n            # remove starts_line for any instructions before the graph break instruction\n            # this will ensure the instructions after the break have the correct line numbers\n            for inst in instructions:\n                if inst.offset == target.offset:\n                    break\n                inst.starts_line = None\n                if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n                    inst.positions = None\n\n            if cleanup:\n                prefix.extend(cleanup)\n                prefix.extend(cls.unreachable_codes(code_options))\n\n            # remap original instructions' exception table entries\n            if old_hook_target_remap:\n                assert is_py311_plus\n                for inst in instructions:\n                    if (\n                        inst.exn_tab_entry\n                        and inst.exn_tab_entry.target in old_hook_target_remap\n                    ):\n                        inst.exn_tab_entry.target = old_hook_target_remap[  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                            inst.exn_tab_entry.target\n                        ]\n\n            # TODO(jansel): add dead code elimination here\n            instructions[:] = prefix + instructions\n\n        new_code, _ = transform_code_object(code, update)\n        ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[new_code] = meta\n        return new_code\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def unreachable_codes(code_options: dict[str, Any]) -> list[Instruction]:\n        \"\"\"Codegen a `raise None` to make analysis work for unreachable code\"\"\"\n        return [\n            create_load_const(None),\n            create_instruction(\"RAISE_VARARGS\", arg=1),\n        ]\n\n    @classmethod\n    def generate_based_on_original_code_object(\n        cls,\n        code: types.CodeType,\n        lineno: int,\n        offset: int,\n        setup_fn_target_offsets: tuple[int, ...],\n        *args: Any,\n    ) -> types.CodeType:\n        \"\"\"\n        This handles the case of generating a resume into code generated\n        to resume something else.  We want to always generate starting\n        from the original code object so that if control flow paths\n        converge we only generated 1 resume function (rather than 2^n\n        resume functions).\n        \"\"\"\n\n        meta: ResumeFunctionMetadata = ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[\n            code\n        ]\n        new_offset = -1\n\n        def find_new_offset(\n            instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\n        ) -> None:\n            nonlocal new_offset\n            (target,) = (i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\n            # match the functions starting at the last instruction as we have added a prefix\n            (new_target,) = (\n                i2\n                for i1, i2 in zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions))\n                if i1 is target\n            )\n            assert target.opcode == new_target.opcode\n            assert new_target.offset is not None\n            new_offset = new_target.offset\n\n        transform_code_object(code, find_new_offset)\n        assert new_offset >= 0\n\n        if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n            # setup_fn_target_offsets currently contains the target offset of\n            # each setup_fn, based on `code`. When we codegen the resume function\n            # based on the original code object, `meta.code`, the offsets in\n            # setup_fn_target_offsets must be based on `meta.code` instead.\n            if new_offset not in meta.block_target_offset_remap:\n                block_target_offset_remap = meta.block_target_offset_remap[\n                    new_offset\n                ] = {}\n\n                def remap_block_offsets(\n                    instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\n                ) -> None:\n                    # NOTE: each prefix block generates exactly one PUSH_EXC_INFO,\n                    # so we can tell which block a prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO belongs to,\n                    # by counting. Then we can use meta.prefix_block-target_offset_remap\n                    # to determine where in the original code the PUSH_EXC_INFO offset\n                    # replaced.\n                    prefix_blocks: list[Instruction] = []\n                    for inst in instructions:\n                        if len(prefix_blocks) == len(\n                            meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\n                        ):\n                            break\n                        if inst.opname == \"PUSH_EXC_INFO\":\n                            prefix_blocks.append(inst)\n\n                    # offsets into prefix\n                    for inst, o in zip(\n                        prefix_blocks, meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\n                    ):\n                        block_target_offset_remap[cast(int, inst.offset)] = o\n\n                    # old bytecode targets are after the prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO's\n                    old_start_offset = (\n                        cast(int, prefix_blocks[-1].offset) if prefix_blocks else -1\n                    )\n                    # offsets into old bytecode\n                    old_inst_offsets = sorted(\n                        n for n in setup_fn_target_offsets if n > old_start_offset\n                    )\n                    targets = _filter_iter(\n                        instructions, old_inst_offsets, lambda inst, o: inst.offset == o\n                    )\n                    new_targets = _filter_iter(\n                        zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions)),\n                        targets,\n                        lambda v1, v2: v1[0] is v2,\n                    )\n                    for new, old in zip(new_targets, targets):\n                        block_target_offset_remap[old.offset] = new[1].offset\n\n                transform_code_object(code, remap_block_offsets)\n\n            # if offset is not in setup_fn_target_offsets, it is an error\n            setup_fn_target_offsets = tuple(\n                meta.block_target_offset_remap[new_offset][n]\n                for n in setup_fn_target_offsets\n            )\n        return ContinueExecutionCache.lookup(\n            meta.code, lineno, new_offset, setup_fn_target_offsets, *args\n        )\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}
